meta.scope: Full dev-E2E of Tessera enforce write-cycle + CXR-02 + #11 D9-ack on real HA
meta.tessera_ref: main (1e2e817), current-main synced into the disposable dev volume
meta.method: fresh onboard (owner token, scratchpad-only) -> seed role/policy -> options-flow mode changes -> matrix WS / services -> hard-checks read from .storage/auth (in-memory via config/auth/list to avoid the auth-store debounce lag) and tessera.state
meta.safety: dev-only, disposable ha-tessera-dev-config volume, no /Volumes/config bind; .storage/auth backed up to scratchpad baseline before enforce; maison-atrium-dev untouched; no tokens/passwords in this evidence

scenario.test_user: E2E Tester (non-admin, realistic system-users membership per QUALITY.md R2)

step5a_cxr02_live_reapply.note: native tessera:viewer policy reflected the panel change on the first read (no reload)

corrected_assumptions.assumption: managed non-admin keeps system-users AND gains tessera:viewer (superset)
corrected_assumptions.reality: managed non-admin is MOVED out of system-users into the restrictive allow-only tessera:viewer group; only system-admin/system-read-only are preserved
corrected_assumptions.verdict: behaviour is correct by design; the initial E2E expectation was wrong

corrected_assumptions.assumption: pre_install_snapshot should capture the owner too
corrected_assumptions.reality: snapshot captures managed users only (owner + system_generated are skipped); restore skips them regardless
corrected_assumptions.verdict: behaviour is correct by design; the initial E2E expectation was wrong

observations.detail: A D9 block (or any unsafe path) persists mode=monitor to the config and files a Repairs issue (_fail_safe_to_monitor). An admin ack alone does not re-enter enforce; the admin must deliberately re-set enforce after acking. This is a safe, intentional flow.

observations.topic: stale native state after a post-apply fail-safe block
observations.detail: If enforce applied successfully once and a later re-evaluation is blocked by D9, the fail-safe drops to monitor but leaves the last-applied (more restrictive) tessera groups in place until the admin resolves and re-runs a clean enforce->off cycle. Not a security downgrade (state is the last-good, more restrictive); a minor UX inconsistency (monitor shown while native tessera groups persist). Recommend documenting; not a blocker.

flush_note: HA debounces .storage/auth writes; user-group changes lag the file by ~1s. All user-group hard-checks were read from the in-memory config/auth/list (authoritative). Group policies flushed immediately in practice.
verdict: PASS - enforce write-cycle, CXR-02 live re-apply, and #11 D9-ack services all behave correctly on real HA 2026.6.4; restore returns the auth store byte-exact to baseline.
